div1.entryFree:
Malignant, 1) full of malice: thou liest, m. thing, Tp. I, 2, 257. a m. and a turbaned Turk, Oth. V, 2, 353.
2) injurious, pernicious, hostile: unless the next word have some m. power upon my life, Gent. III, 1, 238. hearing your high majesty is touched with that m. cause, All's II, 1, 114 (== disease). O m. and ill-boding stars, H6A IV, 5, 6. cracked in pieces by m. death, R3 II, 2, 52. to your person his will is most m. H8 I, 2, 141.
